ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Continued To Dec-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Creditor, Hidden Valley Lake Association, To Allow Late Filing Of Claim Against Estate Of Alexandra V. Balitsky
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jacqueline Vinaccia for (petitioner) Hidden Valley Lake Association. V. Winnie Tungpagasit for (administrator/respondent) Peter Balitsky.

The Court confirmed that the parties are ready to submit, that it boils down to contacts between the administrator/representative and creditor/representative, whether it does or does not constitute the notice requirements of 9103, that if so the claim is not made and if it does not qualify it will not allow a late claim. Ms. Vinaccia argued that it was up to the executor to provide proper notice, that it had to be in writing, that there is a conflict between the law of Probate and the laws of debt collection, that there are strict procedures that are very clear and that they did not receive an affidavit of death of the title holder. The Court stated that the claimant had a form of notice, that they can file a claim without written authorization, that the statute does not speak to the filing of a claim, that the claimant was told of the death of the title holder as well as the death of the successor and that a probate was opened and that it sounds like notice. Ms. Vinaccia stated that notice was not given nor documents provided, that 9103 should be read in conjunction with the other laws and that notice is not a random phone call. The Court stated that the code does not say written notice it says actual notice. Ms. Tungpagasit stated that 9103 mentions actual knowledge and that the creditor did get this in May. The Court asked if title has been transferred from the trustee to Ms. Balitsky. Ms. Tungpagasit stated that it will eventually be an asset of the estate. The Court stated that the claim is in respect to property that is not in the estate and denied the petition of Hidden Valley Lake Association to allow a late filing of claim against the Estate of Alexandra V. Balitsky stating that it does not satisfy 9103.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-08-2016 Continued To Nov-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2131 33rd Avenue, Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Winnie Tungpagasit for and with Peter Balitsky.

The sale for 100% interest was confirmed at $1,019,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 2/24/16. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt/review of the order). An additional bond in an amount to be determined is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2131 33rd Avenue, Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Mar-15-2016 Continued To Mar-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Lubarsky for Peter Balitsky.

The Court stated that there are procedural issues in that there is no proof of publication. Mr. Lubarsky stated that they were deficient but that some courts go forward anyway. The Court stated that this will have to be continued, that it is a statutory requirement, that there may be other bidders, asked the buyer & buyers agent (who were present in court) if they were prepared to waive notice and which they agreed to and asked the extent of encumbrances on the property. Mr. Lubarsky stated that it is $232,000.00. The Court stated that they may need additional bond and that they can shorten time for publication. The Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property was continued to March 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the administrator to comply with the requirement of notice and proof to the court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos & Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Lubarsky for (petitioner) Peter Balitsky. Michael Fox for Geroge Benny.

Mr. Fox stated that they have not agreed on a neutral party. Mr. Lubarsly reported that there are concerns because Mr. Benny has been convicted of fraud, that the substantial property is the home of the parents of the decedent and petitioner, that Mr. Benny was separated from the decedent for many years, that there is property in the home belonging to the decedent and the parents, that Mr. Balitsky has been approved for bonding and that if the appointment of Mr. Balitsky is rejected they propose having Mr. Balitsky and Mr. Benny serve together. Mr. Fox reported that Mr. Balitsky has had exclusive access to the house even though everyone was advised to go together, that they know he has been in the house more than once, that they do not know what he has done in the house, that the house is a disaster/pack rat house and in disrepair, that the spouse has priority over a sibling, that Mr. Benny has served his time, that Mr. Benny has been approved for bonding, that the estate has no cash, that if Mr. Benny is not appointed they would like a professional appointed and that there is significant distrust between the parties and that they would not be able to work together. The two petitions for letters of administration were taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Continued To Oct-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Lubarsky for (proposed administrator) Peter Balitsky. Walt Shjeflo for (proposed administrator) George Benny.

The Court asked if a neutral administrator is a possibility. Mr. Lubarsky stated that Mr. Shjeflo's client did not live with the decedent, spoke of Mr. Benny's past history and concerns that he cannot be bonded, that there is no will, that Mr. Balitsky is the brother of the decedent, that the sole property is a house, that there are 50 boxes remaining to go through and they may find the will and that if Mr. Benny is not removed then there should be consideration of a co-administrator. Mr. Shjeflo stated that a conviction is not a bar to being appointed administrator or executor, that the issue happened a long time ago, that Mr. Benny has served his time, that he can be bonded, that he has statutory priority, that Mr. Benny feels he has a fiduciary duty to do things well, that another piece of property has surfaced as a result of the deposition on Monday. The two petitions for letters of administration were continued to October 5, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to agree on a neutral administrator or the matter will be submitted on that date. Mr. Shjeflo stated that they can try team building and go through the remaining documents and that they will try to come up with something.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
